label: Tesla Founder Mismatch (High Risk)
description: Checks if the founder names are correctly flagged as conflicting, since Elon Musk did not start the company, but Martin Eberhard and Marc Tarpenning did.
question: Who founded Tesla?
llm_answer: Tesla was founded by Elon Musk in 2003.
sample_answers: Tesla was founded in 2003 by Martin Eberhard and Marc Tarpenning.
Tesla Motors was started by Martin Eberhard and Marc Tarpenning.
Elon Musk joined Tesla later as an investor and chairman.

label: Apple Founding (Low Risk)
description: Tests a highly consistent fact. All founders, the year, and details match perfectly across all samples.
question: When was Apple founded?
llm_answer: Apple was founded in 1976 by Steve Jobs, Steve Wozniak, and Ronald Wayne.
sample_answers: Apple was founded in 1976 by Steve Jobs, Steve Wozniak, and Ronald Wayne.
Apple Computer was started in 1976 by Steve Jobs, Steve Wozniak, and Ronald Wayne.

label: OpenAI HQ (Medium/Low Risk)
description: Tests a case where the samples offer slightly different level of details, but are consistent with the original claim (San Francisco, 2015).
question: Where is OpenAI headquartered?
llm_answer: OpenAI is headquartered in San Francisco and was founded in 2015.
sample_answers: OpenAI was founded in 2015 and is based in San Francisco.
OpenAI is an AI research company founded in 2015.
OpenAI is headquartered in San Francisco, California.

label: Seattle Population (Numeric Contradiction)
description: Checks whether a claim containing a wrong numeric figure (1.2 million) is flagged against samples that state the correct figure (~730k-750k).
question: What is the population of Seattle?
llm_answer: Seattle has a population of 1.2 million people.
sample_answers: Seattle has a population of about 730,000 people.
The city of Seattle has a population of roughly 740,000 as of recent estimates.

label: Titanic Sinking (Date Mismatch)
description: Checks if the system catches the wrong year (1915 vs 1912) for a major historical event.
question: When did the Titanic sink?
sample_answers: The Titanic sank in the early morning of April 15, 1912.
The RMS Titanic hit an iceberg and sank on April 15, 1912.
It sank on April 15, 1912, in the North Atlantic Ocean.
